label: Implement
tagline: Deploy the training and support learners through the experience.

whatItIs.beginner: Implementation is when you actually deliver or launch the training to real learners. For e-learning, this means uploading it to the LMS and enrolling learners. For instructor-led training, it means facilitating the session. This phase also includes making sure everything works, supporting learners during the experience, and communicating to managers and stakeholders that the training is live.
whatItIs.advanced: The Implement phase encompasses deployment, facilitation, and learner support. For e-learning, this includes LMS configuration (enrollment, completion rules, reporting settings, due dates), learner communication campaigns, and manager briefings. For ILT/VILT, it includes facilitator preparation, logistics coordination, co-facilitation, and real-time troubleshooting. A pilot session with a representative sample before full rollout is a best practice that catches issues before they affect the entire audience.

whyItMatters.beginner: Even perfect training can fail if implementation is messy. Learners who can't access the course, managers who don't know training exists, or facilitators who aren't prepared can all derail a project. Good implementation ensures the right people get the right training at the right time — and that it actually works.
whyItMatters.advanced: Implementation is where instructional design meets change management. Learner motivation, manager support, and environmental readiness significantly impact training effectiveness. A well-planned implementation includes a communication plan (who tells learners what, when, and why), manager activation (coaching managers on their role in reinforcing learning), and a support plan (help desk, FAQ, office hours). Without these elements, completion rates drop and transfer to the job suffers.

keyQuestions: Is the training accessible to all intended learners on their devices and in their environment?
Do facilitators feel prepared and have they practiced with the materials?
Have managers been briefed on the training and their role in supporting it?
What is the communication plan for announcing the training to learners?
What support is available to learners who have technical or content questions?
Is a pilot planned before full rollout?
How will completion and participation be tracked?
outputs: Deployed course in LMS (with enrollment, completion rules, and reporting configured)
Facilitator preparation and dry-run session notes
Rollout timeline and completion tracking dashboard

commonMistakes.fix: A pilot with 5–10 representative learners will surface technical issues, confusing content, and timing problems before they affect hundreds of people.

commonMistakes.mistake: Forgetting managers in the communication plan
commonMistakes.fix: Managers are your biggest lever for learning transfer. Brief them before launch so they can prime learners and reinforce post-training.
Test enrollment rules, completion tracking, and reporting before go-live. LMS configurations go wrong constantly.

commonMistakes.mistake: Treating implementation as the end of the project
commonMistakes.fix: Implementation is the handoff to Evaluate. Start collecting data from day one.
Learners who know why training matters complete it faster and transfer more. Include a clear rationale in your learner communication.

advancedInsights.heading: When you can't run a formal pilot
advancedInsights.body: For very small audiences (under 15 people) or urgent regulatory deadlines, replace a formal pilot with a structured alpha test: three representative learners complete the course under direct observation, and you address any critical issues before deployment. Document what you observed and the decisions you made — this protects you if questions arise about the launch quality later.

advancedInsights.heading: LMS-specific SCORM gotchas
advancedInsights.body: In Workday Learning, Cornerstone, SAP SuccessFactors, and most enterprise LMS platforms, test your SCORM package in a sandbox org — not just locally in your authoring tool. Completion trigger settings (slide completion vs. quiz score threshold vs. time spent) vary by LMS and can be silently misconfigured. Always verify that completion is recording correctly in your test account before enrolling the full audience.

advancedInsights.heading: Global rollouts: localization is not just translation
advancedInsights.body: For programs rolling out across multiple countries, localization is a separate project within Implement. Budget time for cultural adaptation review — not just translation. Words translate; idioms, scenarios, and visual metaphors often do not. A manufacturing scenario set in Texas may be confusing or irrelevant to learners in Germany or India. Engage in-country reviewers before finalizing any localized content.

advancedInsights.heading: See also: Agile ID
advancedInsights.body: In Agile ID, Implementation is not a single phase — content is released in sprint cycles as each module is completed. Learners access training progressively rather than waiting for the entire program. This is ideal for large programs where a 100% completion gate before any release would delay learning by weeks or months.

caseStudy.situation: Rather than enrolling all 200 sales reps at once, the Meridian team planned a phased rollout with a structured pilot — a decision that turned out to catch two real problems before they hit the full audience.
caseStudy.approach: The highest-performing region (42 reps) received access first as the pilot group. Managers across all regions received a one-page briefing two weeks before their cohort's launch date, explaining the training purpose, the completion deadline, and specific actions they should take before, during, and after training. LMS enrollment was staggered by region over 3 weeks. On the first morning of the pilot, 6 reps reported they couldn't access the course. Because the launch checklist included a documented technical support contact, the issue was escalated to IT within an hour — an SSO configuration mismatch was resolved within 24 hours.
caseStudy.outcome: The pilot region hit an 82% completion rate at two weeks, above the 75% target. The most common learner support question — "Where do I find the spec comparison tables?" — revealed a navigation gap in the LMS course page. A resources tab was added before the next region went live, and that question never appeared in the support inbox again. The staggered rollout meant the fix reached 158 learners before they ever encountered the problem.
